items.title: Kurdistan Region President Congratulates UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham, Pledges Stronger Ties
items.desc: “The Kurdistan Region and Iraq have strong and historic ties with the UK, and we look forward to working together to advance our shared values,” Kurdistan Region President Barzani wrote.

items.title: PM Barzani Congratulates Andy Burnham on Becoming UK Prime Minister
items.desc: In a post on X, Prime Minister Barzani wrote: "I look forward to working together to further strengthen the close partnership between the Kurdistan Region and the UK."

items.title: President Barzani Backs Inaugural Hejar Mukriyani Congress in Germany
items.desc: Held under President Masoud Barzani's patronage, the inaugural Hejar Mukriyani Congress in Germany will bring together Kurdish writers, scholars, and intellectuals to strengthen literature, preserve cultural heritage, and expand cooperation across the global diaspora.

items.title: Iran's Qalibaf Says Tehran Sees Through 'American Games' as Tensions Deepen
items.desc: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammed Baqer Qalibaf said Tehran judges Washington by its military actions rather than its rhetoric, as regional tensions persist and diplomatic contacts continue through mediators.

items.title: KRG Expands Gashanawa Project With 150 Million Dinar Support for SMEs
items.desc: The Kurdistan Regional Government will launch a new phase of Project Gashanawa before the end of 2026, providing up to 150 million Iraqi dinars for more than 100 eligible small and medium-sized enterprises through a fully digital application and evaluation system.

items.title: Wildlife dilemma puts 134 species at risk in Iraq
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq faces a severe environmental challenge as 134 species are at risk of extinction, the Strategic Center for Human Rights warned on Sunday, citing data from the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN). items.title: President Barzani congratulates Burnham’s appointment as UK PM
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil Kurdistan Region President Nechirvan Barzani on Monday congratulated Andy Burnham on becoming British prime minister, citing longstanding ties between the Kurdistan Region, Iraq, and the United Kingdom. “We look forward to working together to advance our shared values,” President Barzani stated on X. items.title: Iran hits ‘US sites’ in Bahrain and Kuwait
items.desc: Shafaq News- Middle East Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) on Monday claimed strikes on three US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait in the 22nd wave of Operation “Victory 2.” The IRGC stated that it destroyed US drone storage and repair facilities at Bahrain’s Sakhir airport and struck buildings supporting Task Force 59 boats at Mina Salman port, damagi

items.title: Erbil fixes gasoline prices to curb fuel shortage
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil The Kurdistan Regional Government (KRG) began enforcing new gasoline prices under directives from Prime Minister Masrour Barzani to address fuel shortages and ease costs for consumers, Natural Resources Minister Kamal Mohammed Salih said on Monday. items.title: President Barzani highlights UK ties in consul farewell
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil President Nechirvan Barzani on Monday praised outgoing British Consul General Andrew Beasley, who completed his tenure in the Kurdistan Region, for advancing relations between London and Erbil, the presidency said. items.title: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
items.desc: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.

items.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
items.desc: Robbie Williams and Jennifer Hudson also performed, with Kylie Jenner and Timothee Chalamet among the celebrities in the crowds.

items.title: In pictures: Joyous fans gather across the US and Canada to watch World Cup final
items.desc: Spirits were high despite none of the three tournament co-hosts making it to Saturday's nail-biting conclusion.

items.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
items.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

items.title: JD Vance says wife Usha and son 'happy and healthy' after birth of fourth child
items.desc: The couple welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, on Sunday morning.

items.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
items.desc: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

items.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
items.desc: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.desc: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
items.desc: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
items.desc: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

items.title: Trump says US strikes hit Iran in 'honour' of American soldiers killed
items.desc: Iran responded with attacks in Syria and Jordan, as two ships reportedly exploded in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Hamas names Khalil al-Hayya as new overall leader
items.desc: The Palestinian armed group's Qatar-based chief negotiator replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza in 2024.

items.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
items.desc: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.
